title: Pterospermadendron xylocarpum
intro: Pterospermadendron xylocarpum, commonly known as the "Dragon's Blood Tree," is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterospermadendron xylocarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of herbal tablets and capsules.
taxonomy: Pterospermadendron xylocarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Pterospermadendron and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant is a member of the order Malvales and is further categorized under the class Magnoliopsida. Its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other organisms.

side_effects: Pterospermadendron xylocarpum, also known as the Chinese wingnut, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, but there is limited scientific research on its side effects. However, it's known that the plant contains saponins, which can be toxic in large quantities. Ingestion of the plant's seeds or leaves may cause gastrointestinal problems, such as nausea and vomiting.
